title: We read in verse 6, ‘So Jesus went with them. But just before they arrived at the house, the officer sent some friends to say, “Lord, don’t trouble yourself by coming to my home, for I am not worthy of such an honour.’
scripture_ref: ‘’When Jesus had finished saying all this the people who were listening, he entered Capernaum. There a centurion’s servant, whom his master valued highly, was sick and about to die. The centurion heard of Jesus and sent some elders of the Jews to him, asking him to come and heal his servant. When they came to Jesus, they pleaded earnestly with him, “This man deserves to have you do this, because he loves our nation and has built our synagogue.” So Jesus went with them He was not far from the house when the centurion sent friends to say to him: “Lord, don’t trouble yourself, for I do not deserve to have you come under my roof. That is why I did not even consider myself worthy to come to you. But say the word, and my servant will be healed. For I myself am a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I tell this one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and that one, ‘Come,’ and he comes. I say to my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.” When Jesus heard this, he was amazed at him, and turning to the crowd following him, he said, “I tell you, I have not found such great faith even in Israel.” Then the men who had been sent, returned to the house and found the servant well.’’
scripture_text: E. Countercultural living will mean that we have a realistic view of ourselves.
devotional:
Some think the reason why the centurion did not want Jesus to enter his home was because he was a gentile and Jews were not allowed into their homes in case they become unclean. I think it was more to do with the centurion’s self awareness.

Often I have thought how amazing it would be to have Jesus’ physical presence in our home, and it would be, however, the attitude of this centurion was so different than mine. His attitude was one of complete humility, free from any sense of entitlement or any feeling of deserving. His humility was also expressed when he said, ‘Lord don’t trouble yourself.’ Lord don’t put yourself out for me.’ I wonder if we have ever said those words to Jesus?

F. Countercultural living requires an exclusive hope in the authority of Jesus.

‘Just say the word from where you are and my servant will be healed.’ There were many things this centurion did not know or understand about Jesus, but he understood that Jesus had authority. In the face of the impending death of his esteemed slave, this centurion found comfort and hope in the fact that Jesus was a person of great authority, and that authority could make all the difference.

In a world full of chaos, fear and confusion we have the freedom to live distinct countercultural lives because all authority in heaven and earth has been given to Jesus. We can live in humble expectation because the word of Jesus is as powerful now as it was then. We don’t have to become moulded or squeezed by some kind of ‘misshapen’ Christian experience or by the insidious pressures of the world. Because Jesus has authority, we can be different.

I love the fact that this centurion is the only person in the bible who brought the need of a slave to Jesus.

G. Countercultural lives touches the heart of Jesus.

Luke tells us that ‘Jesus was amazed by this man’s faith’. The Greek word for amazed thaumazo, means to wonder, to marvel, to be struck with admiration or astonishment. To be surprised by the unexpected. This may have been one of the sweetest moment of Jesus’ ministry. He had been amazed before at the unbelief of the people of Nazareth see Mark 6:1-7. And he told a Canaanite women that her faith was great, see Matthew 15:28.but this the only time Jesus was amazed by someone’s faith.

This man’s faith was not set of dogmas or theological tenets that he subscribed to. It was much more than a sure confidence in the authority and power of Jesus. Rather it was a confident belief in the authority and power of Jesus that directed and shaped how he lived and how he treated others.

I do not think it is a coincidence that the person whose faith amazed Jesus was a centurion, because to live a life of faith requires great courage in the face of threatening circumstances, just like this centurion. I don’t know about you, but I would love to be ‘atypical,’ not odd, or strange, or awkward, but someone who lives a life shaped and directed by authentic faith.
prayer: Prayer, Father help me to have the courage to have a faith that expresses love for all people.
html:
We read in verse 6, ‘So Jesus went with them. But just before they arrived at the house, the officer sent some friends to say, “Lord, don’t trouble yourself by coming to my home, for I am not worthy of such an honour.’

‘’When Jesus had finished saying all this the people who were listening, he entered Capernaum. There a centurion’s servant, whom his master valued highly, was sick and about to die. The centurion heard of Jesus and sent some elders of the Jews to him, asking him to come and heal his servant. When they came to Jesus, they pleaded earnestly with him, “This man deserves to have you do this, because he loves our nation and has built our synagogue.” So Jesus went with them He was not far from the house when the centurion sent friends to say to him: “Lord, don’t trouble yourself, for I do not deserve to have you come under my roof. That is why I did not even consider myself worthy to come to you. But say the word, and my servant will be healed. For I myself am a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I tell this one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and that one, ‘Come,’ and he comes. I say to my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.” When Jesus heard this, he was amazed at him, and turning to the crowd following him, he said, “I tell you, I have not found such great faith even in Israel.” Then the men who had been sent, returned to the house and found the servant well.’’
E. Countercultural living will mean that we have a realistic view of ourselves.

Some think the reason why the centurion did not want Jesus to enter his home was because he was a gentile and Jews were not allowed into their homes in case they become unclean. I think it was more to do with the centurion’s self awareness.

Often I have thought how amazing it would be to have Jesus’ physical presence in our home, and it would be, however, the attitude of this centurion was so different than mine. His attitude was one of complete humility, free from any sense of entitlement or any feeling of deserving. His humility was also expressed when he said, ‘Lord don’t trouble yourself.’ Lord don’t put yourself out for me.’ I wonder if we have ever said those words to Jesus?

F. Countercultural living requires an exclusive hope in the authority of Jesus.

‘Just say the word from where you are and my servant will be healed.’ There were many things this centurion did not know or understand about Jesus, but he understood that Jesus had authority. In the face of the impending death of his esteemed slave, this centurion found comfort and hope in the fact that Jesus was a person of great authority, and that authority could make all the difference.

In a world full of chaos, fear and confusion we have the freedom to live distinct countercultural lives because all authority in heaven and earth has been given to Jesus. We can live in humble expectation because the word of Jesus is as powerful now as it was then. We don’t have to become moulded or squeezed by some kind of ‘misshapen’ Christian experience or by the insidious pressures of the world. Because Jesus has authority, we can be different.

I love the fact that this centurion is the only person in the bible who brought the need of a slave to Jesus.

G. Countercultural lives touches the heart of Jesus.

Luke tells us that ‘Jesus was amazed by this man’s faith’. The Greek word for amazed thaumazo, means to wonder, to marvel, to be struck with admiration or astonishment. To be surprised by the unexpected. This may have been one of the sweetest moment of Jesus’ ministry. He had been amazed before at the unbelief of the people of Nazareth see Mark 6:1-7. And he told a Canaanite women that her faith was great, see Matthew 15:28.but this the only time Jesus was amazed by someone’s faith.

This man’s faith was not set of dogmas or theological tenets that he subscribed to. It was much more than a sure confidence in the authority and power of Jesus. Rather it was a confident belief in the authority and power of Jesus that directed and shaped how he lived and how he treated others.

I do not think it is a coincidence that the person whose faith amazed Jesus was a centurion, because to live a life of faith requires great courage in the face of threatening circumstances, just like this centurion. I don’t know about you, but I would love to be ‘atypical,’ not odd, or strange, or awkward, but someone who lives a life shaped and directed by authentic faith.

Prayer: Prayer, Father help me to have the courage to have a faith that expresses love for all people.
Bible in a year reading plan: -Judges 8-9
